meaning: n.浮桥,浮筒,浮码头vt.架浮桥于
examples: Have you ever heard of a pontoon bridge?
A stabilizer for a canoe; spars attach to a shaped log or pontoon parallel to the hull.
A German motorized artillery column crossing the Don river by means of a pontoon bridge on July 31, 1942.
Passengers were moved on to a pontoon in accordance with safety procedures, said a tour spokesman.
In the G-21A Grumman Goose floatplane, I have just learned, the passenger compartment is the pontoon.
